EigenInference.claim: The app is a menu-bar-only macOS application that wraps the Rust darkbloom binary with dynamic activation policy management
The app structure includes MenuBarExtra, Settings, Dashboard, Setup, and Log windows with proper scene management
StatusViewModel centralizes all provider state and coordinates between managers and UI components
The view model manages published properties for provider state, hardware info, wallet data, and connectivity
ProviderManager manages the darkbloom binary as a subprocess with output capture and auto-restart capabilities
The manager implements exponential backoff crash recovery with up to 5 restart attempts
CLIRunner centralizes all darkbloom subcommand execution with proper process management
Binary resolution follows a consistent search order: ~/.darkbloom/bin, app bundle, then PATH
MenuBarView provides the main dropdown interface with Liquid Glass effects on macOS 26+
SecurityManager performs comprehensive security posture assessment for hardware trust verification
Hardware trust requires Secure Enclave, MDM enrollment, SIP, and Secure Boot all enabled
ModelManager scans HuggingFace cache directory for MLX models and manages downloads
ConfigManager reads and writes the same provider.toml used by the Rust CLI for consistency
TelemetryReporter ships errors and crash reports to coordinator with bounded buffering and debounced flushing
The component has zero external Swift package dependencies, only targeting macOS 14+
Real-time status polling checks both process existence and backend health on port 8100
Provider output parsing extracts connection status, inference lifecycle, and throughput metrics from tracing logs
AppDelegate manages activation policy switching between accessory and regular modes based on window visibility
Design system provides unified color tokens matching console.darkbloom.dev with light/dark mode support

EigenInferenceEnclave.claim: Swift package structure with library and executable products, targeting macOS 13+
SecureEnclaveIdentity manages hardware-bound P-256 keys with init() constructor
Hardware-isolated signing operation returns DER-encoded signatures
AttestationBlob structure contains comprehensive hardware and security state fields
createAttestation method generates signed attestations with sorted JSON keys
FFI bridge uses @_cdecl annotations and Unmanaged memory management
Sign operation via FFI takes raw data pointer and length, returns base64 signature
CLI tool provides attest command with ephemeral key generation
Hardware detection via system_profiler parsing for chip identification
SIP status checking via csrutil command execution
FFI bridge provides C ABI for Rust integration with memory management conventions
Dependencies on CryptoKit and Foundation system frameworks
Memory management function for freeing C strings returned by FFI
JSON serialization testing with sorted key ordering for deterministic output
FFI bridge testing demonstrates C symbol resolution and memory management

EigenInferenceEnclaveCLI.claim: CLI provides attestation and diagnostic functionality with ephemeral keys
Command-line argument parsing and command dispatch architecture
cmdAttest function generates signed attestation blobs
cmdInfo function provides system diagnostic information
Manual argument parsing with option flag support
Centralized error handling with stderr output and exit codes
JSON encoding with sorted keys for deterministic output
Secure Enclave availability validation before operations
Dependencies on CryptoKit, EigenInferenceEnclave, and Foundation
Uses SecureEnclaveIdentity and AttestationService from EigenInferenceEnclave
CLI supports attest and info commands with specific options
WebSocket bridge functionality was removed due to Apple keychain restrictions
CLI is defined as executable target with dependency on EigenInferenceEnclave

analytics.claim: Service entry point implements structured logging and configuration loading
Service orchestrates component construction through dependency injection
Dual backend support implemented through common Store interface
Configuration structure with backend-specific settings
Backend-specific validation and requirements
Health check endpoint with backend connectivity verification
Overview endpoint for network statistics
Earnings leaderboard endpoint with query parameter validation
CORS middleware implementation with configurable origins
Service layer abstracts storage backends through dependency injection
Business logic for data aggregation and alias generation
MemoryStore with realistic mock data for development
PostgresStore with connection pooling via pgx/v5
PostgreSQL queries for overview statistics
Dynamic SQL generation for leaderboard queries
HMAC-SHA256 based deterministic alias generation
Predefined word lists for human-friendly aliases
Primary dependency on pgx/v5 for PostgreSQL connectivity
Standalone service design for read-only analytics
Database table dependencies for provider and earnings data

coordinator.claim: The coordinator is the central routing and trust layer in the Darkbloom network running in GCP Confidential VM with hardware-encrypted memory
Main function sets up core components including store, registry, server, billing, and rate limiting
Server struct contains registry, store, billing, rate limiters, and various configuration settings
Routes method defines all HTTP endpoints including consumer inference APIs, provider WebSocket, billing endpoints, and admin management
Consumer API implements OpenAI-compatible endpoints with streaming support and configurable timeouts
Provider WebSocket handler manages provider connections with registration and ACME client certificate verification
Registry manages connected providers with trust levels, model catalog, and request queue
E2E encryption uses NaCl Box with X25519 key exchange for request confidentiality between coordinator and providers
Billing service provides unified payment processing with Stripe and referral system
Store interface provides dual-backend storage with MemoryStore for development and PostgreSQL for production
Protocol package defines WebSocket message types for coordinator-provider communication
External dependencies include WebSocket library, PostgreSQL driver, Datadog clients, JWT parsing, and crypto primitives
Datadog integration with APM tracer and client for metrics and logs
Billing configuration includes Stripe payments and Connect Express with referral system
Step-CA root certificate configuration for ACME client cert verification

darkbloom.claim: The provider agent runs on Mac hardware and serves local inference requests from the Darkbloom coordinator
Architecture includes hardware detection, model scanning, backend management, coordinator connection, and Secure Enclave attestation
WebSocket client manages provider's connection to coordinator with automatic reconnection, registration, heartbeats, and inference request handling
Security module implements runtime protections including PT_DENY_ATTACH, SIP verification, and memory wiping to prevent inference data inspection
Hardware detection queries macOS system APIs via sysctl and system_profiler to detect Apple Silicon capabilities and memory bandwidth
Model management scans HuggingFace cache for MLX models and performs SHA-256 weight fingerprinting for attestation
Ephemeral X25519 key pair generated fresh on every provider launch, protected by PT_DENY_ATTACH + Hardened Runtime + SIP
In-process inference engine using embedded Python runs inference inside hardened Rust process with no IPC channel to sniff
Backend management wraps inference engines with health monitoring and automatic restart using exponential backoff
Telemetry architecture includes tracing layer, panic hook, stderr scraper feeding into mpsc queue and async batcher for HTTPS transmission
Service management integrates with launchd as user agent, only running when user explicitly starts via darkbloom start
Configuration system stores TOML format at ~/.config/eigeninference/provider.toml with hardware-adapted defaults
External dependencies include tokio for async runtime, reqwest for HTTP, crypto_box for NaCl encryption, and pyo3 for Python embedding
Component connects to coordinator via WebSocket and downloads models from R2 CDN with configurable URLs
PT_DENY_ATTACH syscall prevents debugger attachment and makes process memory unreadable when combined with SIP and Hardened Runtime
Service serves inference requests in main event loop with security verification, backend management, and coordinator communication
Python path locking restricts imports to only approved runtime roots preventing provider from injecting malicious code
Provider-coordinator protocol includes Registration, Heartbeat, InferenceRequest, and AttestationChallenge message types over WebSocket

decrypt-test.claim: Component is designed for cross-language compatibility testing with NaCl Box decryption
Main function implements argument parsing with manual validation for three required parameters
Base64 decoding operations include comprehensive error handling with descriptive messages
Implements validation for 32-byte keys and minimum 24-byte ciphertext for nonce
Creates X25519 keys and SalsaBox instance for ECDH key exchange
Extracts 24-byte nonce and performs authenticated decryption on remaining ciphertext
Validates decrypted output as UTF-8 before printing to stdout
Uses crypto_box for NaCl operations and base64 for encoding/decoding
Component depends on crypto_box 0.9 and base64 0.22
CLI requires exactly three positional arguments with usage information on failure

verify-attestation.claim: The component reads attestation data from a fixed file path and handles read errors
Uses attestation.VerifyJSON() to parse and verify the attestation blob
Displays hardware information extracted from the attestation
Shows cross-language verification success message and exits with appropriate code
The attestation package provides P-256 ECDSA signature verification with cross-language JSON compatibility
VerifyJSON function provides the main entry point for attestation verification from raw JSON
AttestationBlob struct contains hardware identity and security state fields in alphabetical order
VerificationResult struct provides structured output for verification results
Verify function implements P-256 ECDSA signature verification with security policy checks
Security policy enforcement requires Secure Enclave, SIP, and Secure Boot to be enabled
ParseP256PublicKey handles both 64-byte and 65-byte uncompressed point formats
marshalSortedJSON provides cross-language JSON compatibility with Swift's sorted key encoding
Component is part of the coordinator Go module using Go 1.25.0
Uses golang.org/x/crypto for extended cryptographic operations

web.claim: The component is a Next.js TypeScript package with React 19 and comprehensive dependencies for chat, payments, and encryption
The application uses a provider-based architecture with authentication, theme, verification mode, and telemetry providers
The main chat page implements streaming chat with state management, authentication checks, and model loading
The API client supports optional end-to-end encryption using X25519/NaCl box with coordinator key management
Request sealing uses ephemeral X25519 keypairs and NaCl box encryption for forward secrecy
Authentication uses Privy for email login with automatic API key provisioning from the coordinator
State management uses Zustand with persistence middleware for chat history and preferences
Hardware verification fetches Apple certificate chains and performs X.509 validation against Apple Root CA
Provider dashboard fetches machine status and earnings data from the coordinator API
Billing system integrates with Stripe for payments and coordinator for balance/usage tracking
API routes proxy requests to the coordinator while preserving streaming and encryption headers
Telemetry system batches events with debouncing and uses sendBeacon for reliable delivery
AppShell provides the main layout with sidebar navigation and toast notification system
Chat flow implements streaming with trust metadata, performance metrics, and error handling
Streaming chat handles sealed responses with SSE event decryption and think-block parsing
Trust badges display hardware attestation levels with Apple MDA verification status
Next.js configuration handles TypeScript build issues with viem dependency tree
Models API flattens metadata and processes attestation information for UI display
Authentication route provisions API keys using Privy tokens or Authorization headers
Coordinator key caching uses URL-keyed storage with 1-hour TTL and rotation handling
